---
title: "Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells"
---

# Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells

Range: `1513-1582`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 20    |
| stub     | 50    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                       | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                         |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------ | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1513  | `STUB` [anomaly](#rune-1513)               | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1514  | `STUB` [backlog collapse](#rune-1514)      | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1515  | `STUB` [broken pipe](#rune-1515)           | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1516  | `STUB` [bug](#rune-1516)                   | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1517  | [cascading failure](#rune-1517)            | authored   | One failure fans out into many dependent failures.                                                                                              |
| 1518  | `STUB` [checksum mismatch](#rune-1518)     | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1519  | `STUB` [collision](#rune-1519)             | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1520  | [contention](#rune-1520)                   | authored   | Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.                                                                                          |
| 1521  | [corruption](#rune-1521)                   | authored   | Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.                                                                                             |
| 1522  | `STUB` [crash](#rune-1522)                 | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1523  | `STUB` [curse of shared state](#rune-1523) | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1524  | `STUB` [data drift](#rune-1524)            | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1525  | [deadlock {Curse}](#rune-1525)             | authored   | Two or more actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                 |
| 1526  | `STUB` [defect](#rune-1526)                | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1527  | `STUB` [degraded mode](#rune-1527)         | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1528  | `STUB` [denial of service](#rune-1528)     | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1529  | [dependency hell](#rune-1529)              | authored   | Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.                                                                                    |
| 1530  | `STUB` [divergence](#rune-1530)            | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1531  | [drift](#rune-1531)                        | authored   | Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.                                                                                    |
| 1532  | `STUB` [error](#rune-1532)                 | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1533  | `STUB` [exception](#rune-1533)             | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1534  | [exhaustion](#rune-1534)                   | authored   | The resource is simply used up.                                                                                                                 |
| 1535  | `STUB` [failure domain](#rune-1535)        | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1536  | `STUB` [fault](#rune-1536)                 | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1537  | [flapping](#rune-1537)                     | authored   | Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.                                                                                                     |
| 1538  | `STUB` [fragmentation](#rune-1538)         | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1539  | `STUB` [freeze](#rune-1539)                | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1540  | `STUB` [hang](#rune-1540)                  | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1541  | `STUB` [hotspot](#rune-1541)               | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1542  | [inconsistent read](#rune-1542)            | authored   | A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.                                                                      |
| 1543  | `STUB` [infinite loop](#rune-1543)         | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1544  | `STUB` [invalid state](#rune-1544)         | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1545  | `STUB` [jitter spike](#rune-1545)          | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1546  | [leak {Curse}](#rune-1546)                 | authored   | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.                    |
| 1547  | [livelock](#rune-1547)                     | authored   | Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.                                                                                                |
| 1548  | [lost update](#rune-1548)                  | authored   | One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.                                                                                        |
| 1549  | `STUB` [memory corruption](#rune-1549)     | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1550  | `STUB` [mismatch](#rune-1550)              | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1551  | `STUB` [misroute](#rune-1551)              | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1552  | `STUB` [missed heartbeat](#rune-1552)      | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1553  | `STUB` [null dereference](#rune-1553)      | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1554  | [outage](#rune-1554)                       | authored   | Service unavailable where availability was expected.                                                                                            |
| 1555  | [overflow](#rune-1555)                     | authored   | Value exceeds the representable range.                                                                                                          |
| 1556  | `STUB` [packet loss {Curse}](#rune-1556)   | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1557  | `STUB` [panic](#rune-1557)                 | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1558  | [partial failure](#rune-1558)              | authored   | Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.                                                                                          |
| 1559  | `STUB` [partition storm](#rune-1559)       | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1560  | `STUB` [poison pill](#rune-1560)           | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1561  | [race](#rune-1561)                         | authored   | An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.                |
| 1562  | [regression](#rune-1562)                   | authored   | A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.                                                                                           |
| 1563  | `STUB` [resource starvation](#rune-1563)   | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1564  | [retry storm](#rune-1564)                  | authored   | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.                  |
| 1565  | `STUB` [rollback failure](#rune-1565)      | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1566  | `STUB` [saturation](#rune-1566)            | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1567  | [schema drift](#rune-1567)                 | authored   | The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done. |
| 1568  | [split-brain {Curse}](#rune-1568)          | authored   | Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.                                                                                          |
| 1569  | `STUB` [stack overflow](#rune-1569)        | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1570  | `STUB` [stale read](#rune-1570)            | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1571  | `STUB` [stall](#rune-1571)                 | stub       | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                      |
| 1572  | `STUB` [starvation](#rune-1572)            | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1573  | `STUB` [state explosion](#rune-1573)       | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1574  | `STUB` [storm](#rune-1574)                 | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |
| 1575  | `STUB` [thrash](#rune-1575)                | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1576  | `STUB` [timeout {Curse}](#rune-1576)       | stub       | A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.                                                                          |
| 1577  | `STUB` [torn write](#rune-1577)            | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1578  | `STUB` [underflow](#rune-1578)             | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1579  | `STUB` [unhandled exception](#rune-1579)   | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1580  | `STUB` [unstable equilibrium](#rune-1580)  | stub       | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                        |
| 1581  | `STUB` [write amplification](#rune-1581)   | stub       | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                            |
| 1582  | `STUB` [write skew {Curse}](#rune-1582)    | stub       | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                |

## Entries

### [1513] anomaly {#rune-1513}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating anomaly as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1514] backlog collapse {#rune-1514}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating backlog collapse as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1515] broken pipe {#rune-1515}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating broken pipe as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1516] bug {#rune-1516}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating bug as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1517] cascading failure {#rune-1517}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One failure fans out into many dependent failures.

**Shadow:** treating cascading failure as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** One failure fans out into many dependent failures.

### [1518] checksum mismatch {#rune-1518}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating checksum mismatch as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1519] collision {#rune-1519}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating collision as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1520] contention {#rune-1520}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.

**Shadow:** treating contention as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Too many actors fighting for the same scarce resource.

### [1521] corruption {#rune-1521}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.

**Shadow:** treating corruption as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Stored or transmitted truth damaged into falsehood.

### [1522] crash {#rune-1522}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating crash as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1523] curse of shared state {#rune-1523}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating curse of shared state as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1524] data drift {#rune-1524}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating data drift as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1525] deadlock {Curse} {#rune-1525}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Two or more actors wait on one another forever.

**Shadow:** treating deadlock as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Two or more actors wait on one another forever.

### [1526] defect {#rune-1526}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating defect as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1527] degraded mode {#rune-1527}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating degraded mode as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1528] denial of service {#rune-1528}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating denial of service as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1529] dependency hell {#rune-1529}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.

**Shadow:** treating dependency hell as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Transitive requirements turning ordinary change into a trap.

### [1530] divergence {#rune-1530}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating divergence as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1531] drift {#rune-1531}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.

**Shadow:** treating drift as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Slow departure from the world you thought you were still in.

### [1532] error {#rune-1532}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating error as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1533] exception {#rune-1533}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating exception as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1534] exhaustion {#rune-1534}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The resource is simply used up.

**Shadow:** treating exhaustion as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** The resource is simply used up.

### [1535] failure domain {#rune-1535}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating failure domain as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1536] fault {#rune-1536}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating fault as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1537] flapping {#rune-1537}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.

**Shadow:** treating flapping as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Rapid unhealthy oscillation between states.

### [1538] fragmentation {#rune-1538}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating fragmentation as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1539] freeze {#rune-1539}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating freeze as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1540] hang {#rune-1540}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating hang as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1541] hotspot {#rune-1541}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating hotspot as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1542] inconsistent read {#rune-1542}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.

**Shadow:** treating inconsistent read as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** A read that sees a world that never really existed as one coherent moment.

### [1543] infinite loop {#rune-1543}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating infinite loop as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1544] invalid state {#rune-1544}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating invalid state as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1545] jitter spike {#rune-1545}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating jitter spike as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1546] leak {Curse} {#rune-1546}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.

**Shadow:** when treating a leak as the cause prevents finding the owner, lifetime, or boundary.

**Pocket gloss:** Something escapes the boundary that should have held it.

### [1547] livelock {#rune-1547}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.

**Shadow:** treating livelock as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Everyone keeps moving but nobody makes progress.

### [1548] lost update {#rune-1548}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.

**Shadow:** treating lost update as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** One writer quietly stomps another writer's valid change.

### [1549] memory corruption {#rune-1549}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating memory corruption as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1550] mismatch {#rune-1550}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating mismatch as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1551] misroute {#rune-1551}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating misroute as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1552] missed heartbeat {#rune-1552}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating missed heartbeat as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1553] null dereference {#rune-1553}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating null dereference as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1554] outage {#rune-1554}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Service unavailable where availability was expected.

**Shadow:** treating outage as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Service unavailable where availability was expected.

### [1555] overflow {#rune-1555}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Value exceeds the representable range.

**Shadow:** treating overflow as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Value exceeds the representable range.

### [1556] packet loss {Curse} {#rune-1556}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating packet loss as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1557] panic {#rune-1557}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating panic as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1558] partial failure {#rune-1558}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.

**Shadow:** treating partial failure as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Some of the system failed while the rest kept limping.

### [1559] partition storm {#rune-1559}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating partition storm as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1560] poison pill {#rune-1560}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating poison pill as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1561] race {#rune-1561}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.

**Shadow:** when the race is patched locally while the shared state contract remains undefined.

**Pocket gloss:** Outcome depends on timing you do not actually control.

### [1562] regression {#rune-1562}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

**Shadow:** treating regression as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** A previously solved bug or quality loss has returned.

### [1563] resource starvation {#rune-1563}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating resource starvation as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1564] retry storm {#rune-1564}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.

**Shadow:** when retries synchronize into an outage amplifier rather than a recovery path.

**Pocket gloss:** Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it.

### [1565] rollback failure {#rune-1565}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating rollback failure as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1566] saturation {#rune-1566}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating saturation as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1567] schema drift {#rune-1567}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.

**Shadow:** when schema changes are shipped without compatibility checks, migration windows, or readers.

**Pocket gloss:** Stored shape and assumed shape have silently diverged.

### [1568] split-brain {Curse} {#rune-1568}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.

**Shadow:** treating split-brain as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

**Pocket gloss:** Separated leaders each believe they are authoritative.

### [1569] stack overflow {#rune-1569}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating stack overflow as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1570] stale read {#rune-1570}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating stale read as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1571] stall {#rune-1571}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.

**Shadow:** treating stall as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1572] starvation {#rune-1572}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating starvation as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1573] state explosion {#rune-1573}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating state explosion as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1574] storm {#rune-1574}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating storm as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1575] thrash {#rune-1575}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating thrash as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1576] timeout {Curse} {#rune-1576}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.

**Shadow:** treating timeout as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1577] torn write {#rune-1577}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating torn write as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1578] underflow {#rune-1578}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating underflow as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1579] unhandled exception {#rune-1579}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating unhandled exception as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1580] unstable equilibrium {#rune-1580}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.

**Shadow:** treating unstable equilibrium as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1581] write amplification {#rune-1581}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.

**Shadow:** treating write amplification as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.

### [1582] write skew {Curse} {#rune-1582}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.

**Shadow:** treating write skew as the root cause too early can freeze the investigation around a symptom.
